description:
Most investors judge income ETFs by the price chart.
That’s the wrong metric.
Here’s why total return changes everything.

In this video, I sit down with Michael Khouw — Wall Street veteran, longtime CNBC contributor, co-author of The Options Edge, and the strategist behind YieldMax — to break down why total return is the only honest way to judge income strategies.

If an ETF is built to generate distributions…

You don’t evaluate it like a growth stock.
You evaluate it like a business.

Here’s what we cover:
• Price return vs total return (and why most broker platforms default to price)
• Why income ETFs can look weak on a chart but still deliver performance
• How option-income ETFs like YMAX and ULTY actually generate cash flow
• What NAV erosion really means
• The difference between distribution yield and total return
• How institutions evaluate performance vs how retail investors do
• Market rotation, volatility, and allocation shifts

If you’re holding option-income ETFs — YMAX, ULTY, NVDY — or researching dividend income investing, passive income, financial independence, or covered call ETFs, this conversation will change how you read your own portfolio.

For those new here:
I run my brokerage account like an operating system.
Every paycheck gets invested.
Growth builds equity.
Income strategies generate cash flow.
Margin bridges timing gaps.

Everything is evaluated through a total return lens.
Not just price.
Not just yield.
The full picture.

This isn’t about defending a fund.
This isn’t about hype.

It’s about understanding how income investing actually works.

If you’ve ever wondered:

Why is my ETF price down if it’s paying me?
Are high distributions destroying value?
How should you measure dividend performance?
What does total return actually include?
Is yield erosion real — or misunderstood?

This is the full breakdown.

Drop a comment:

What income ETF should we analyze next through the total return lens?

If you’re serious about running your portfolio like a business — subscribe. We go deep here.

⏱️ Chapters
Chapters
00:00 – YieldMax Updates & Strategy Changes
01:38 – How YieldMax Is Evolving
07:04 – Correlation, Market Rotation & Risk
11:19 – Building Non-Correlated Income Portfolios
15:44 – Total Return vs Price (The Critical Difference)
24:54 – How We Evaluate Stocks
29:20 – Identifying High-Growth Opportunities
32:39 – Refining Stock Selection Criteria
35:25 – Why Analyst Estimates Matter
37:37 – The Holly Index: Consumer Data & Investing
41:47 – From Trading Desk to CNBC
49:49 – Timeless Investment Lessons
52:25 – Learning Options Trading the Right Way


🔥 RESOURCES
🔥 Free Starter Kit + Simple FIRE Calculator

👉 https://paycheckportfolio.gumroad.com/l/iescv
🧤 White Glove Setup + Personal Coaching
👉 https://paycheckportfolio.gumroad.com/l/stfkj

Inside the discord community, members are running this system across Fidelity, Schwab, E*TRADE, Robinhood, M1, Interactive Brokers, and more.

This is not financial advice. I’m sharing what I personally do and how I think about total return, income investing, margin management, dividend cash flow, and financial independence. Always do your own research before investing.

#paychecktoportfolio #incomeinvesting #totalreturn #yieldmax #dividendincome #cashflowinvesting #optionincome #financialindependence #etfs #coveredcalls #passiveincome
